description: B-11 (#3804) macro per-phase wall-clock baseline for the B-7 golden corpus gate. Complements the B-12 graph/query snapshot (e2e-20repo-snapshot.json): the snapshot is the logic contract, this is the performance contract. The golden-corpus-gate compares each live run's phase-timings.json against these numbers and fails a gated phase that regresses beyond the band, OR keeps it advisory on shared CI runners (see regression_policy).

collect.note: cassette replay + the fixed GATE_COLLECTOR_SETTLE_SECONDS settle window + facts-landed verification. Dominated by the configured settle constant, not pipeline work, so it is recorded for visibility but not gated.

maintenance_drains.note: two deferred maintenance + drain cycles (the additive-correlation convergence long pole at full corpus scale)

graph_query.note: eshu-api + eshu-mcp-server startup before the assertion phase (excludes the gate's own assertion time)

regression_policy.rule: A gated phase passes if observed_seconds <= baseline_seconds * (1 + regression_band) OR observed_seconds <= baseline_seconds + absolute_slack_seconds.
regression_policy.ci_mode: The golden-corpus-gate workflow runs the per-phase check in ADVISORY mode (-phase-regression-advisory) because GitHub's shared runners vary run-to-run by more than the band — the same reasoning behind the gate's 2x total-wall-time budget multiplier. Per-PR regressions stay visible without false reds.
regression_policy.blocking_mode: A controlled validation host (consistent hardware) runs with GATE_PHASE_REGRESSION_ADVISORY=false to make the gated phases blocking, where the baseline below is valid.

capture.host_class: developer laptop (apple silicon, arm64), local docker compose
capture.note: Seed capture. Integer-second resolution (date +%s, portable across GNU/BSD); the absolute_slack_seconds allowance absorbs the resulting jitter on small phases. Recapture on the enforcement host for blocking use.

update_protocol.when: Update when an intentional perf change alters a phase's wall-clock, or when recapturing on a new enforcement host. Do not update in response to transient CI noise (that is what the band + slack absorb).
update_protocol.how: Run scripts/refresh-e2e-baseline.sh on the enforcement host, review the diff, and commit with a clear before/after explanation in the PR.
update_protocol.who: Baseline updates require the same review bar as code changes. Silently widening numbers to mask a regression is not acceptable.
